summary: We are in full execution mode on three major initiatives: the Asia Start campaign (launching this week with two test routes), the Intelligent China program (website launching this week, December module in Beijing), and the joint CKGSB-IE 'Going Global' report (targeting September launch in Singapore), while simultaneously prioritizing SEO for Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships.
summaryDetail: The most urgent priority is launching the Asia Start campaign with two test routes (AI and traditional) and the Intelligent China program website this week — both are critical for driving traffic and conversions. The joint report with IE, 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global,' is progressing well: the team is translating the Chinese report into English, collecting alumni case studies, and finalizing a one-pager proposal; the launch is targeted at either Tech in Asia (Sept 15-16) or Tyson event (Sept 29-30) in Singapore. On the SEO front, we've decided to prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China over US partnerships, with Joseph drafting areas to show up in search results. Meanwhile, the social media agency search has concluded — we're sticking with Media Minds due to budget and time constraints — and the Shun Tian Shu press release will be a single combined version. Upcoming this week: Julie needs to provide feedback on menu categorization and adjust brochure figures, Xuan is following up with social media agencies, and the team must translate sections of the going global report. The Women in Leadership Forum speaker review and RFP feedback for social media ad agency selection are due by July 3.

meetings.title: SEO and Program Prioritization
meetings.summary: Team discusses SEO strategy to rank for key topics like AI and EVs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed feedback on menu categorization, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on agency direction and press release approach.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report
meetings.summary: Team discussed joint report between CKGSB and IE on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with op-eds from both schools' leadership and industry case studies. Aim to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' with a December 1-4 module in Beijing.

meetings.title: China-Europe Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on China-Europe relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; will be key for driving traffic.
Stick with Media Minds as social media agency due to small budget and time constraints.
Do not write two separate press releases for Shun Tian Shu; combine into one.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE, with main research from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both deans and possibly President Santiago.
